title: Improvement selection
principles: Every improvement must be traceable to a supplied strength, limitation, or supported role.
Reinforce means increasing a parameter that already supports the selected role or a demonstrated strength.
Correct friction means reducing a demonstrated operational limitation without redefining the weapon's intrinsic mechanic.
Critical chance and critical multiplier reinforce a supported critical profile; neither should be used to correct reload, ammunition, accuracy, or handling friction.
Status chance reinforces supported status application but does not correct target contact, ammunition, or reload friction.
Base damage is a general reinforcement parameter and must never be labeled as correcting operational friction.
Fire rate can reinforce repeated pressure or application frequency, but it may worsen magazine, reload, or ammunition pressure.
Multishot can reinforce delivery instances when valid, but it must not be described as an ammunition-efficiency correction.
Magazine size can reinforce sustained pressure or correct frequent reload interruption by extending the active firing window.
Reload time corrects supported reload friction by shortening downtime.
Accuracy can correct supported contact or spread friction only when accuracy-related evidence exists.
Use none when no allowed parameter has a supported and semantically valid relationship.

reinforce_strength: Choose a parameter already aligned with the supported role or strength.
correct_operational_friction: Choose only a parameter that directly reduces the supplied friction.
tradeoff_required: The improvement may reinforce one function while worsening another supplied limitation.
The reason must mention the relevant tradeoff.
none: No allowed parameter has sufficient evidence or semantic compatibility.

exceptions: Do not pair base_damage with correct_friction.
Do not pair critical or status parameters with correct_friction.
Do not recommend accuracy when no accuracy, spread, recoil, or contact limitation is supplied.
Do not recommend fire rate as a pure improvement when reload or ammunition pressure is already elevated.
Do not recommend changing chaining, radial behavior, charge behavior, burst count, or another intrinsic mechanic.
Do not use a parameter absent from allowed_improvement_parameters.
